rationale: The cited paper is a strong conceptual/mechanism match: it shows marketing cues (price/claims) change product efficacy via expectancy mediation, while the citing paper tests how salient effectiveness cues change perceived effectiveness and usage via inference/mediation. Given this close overlap in focal constructs (marketing cues  perceived/effective outcomes and expectancy-like mechanisms), this is plausibly a foundational theory source rather than an obvious incidental mention, despite only one DOI-level matched reference record.
